Name: Carl Clark
Interesting A: First Black business to clean Kansas City City Hall (~49 employees).
Interesting B: Lifelong Chiefs season ticket holder since 1963. Wife's social circle overlapped with Buck Buchanan's wife through Jackson County Links.
Family / Wife: Wife: Mary Lynn. Son: Carl Jr. Stepfather ran Safety First Hat Works at 18th & Vine.
Career: Career machinist and labor worker at Colgate-Palmolive (long tenure). Started in labor and rose through apprenticeship program. St. James United Methodist Church parishioner.

Name: Jim Nunnelly (James Nunnelly)
Interesting A: One of the first ~15 African Americans admitted to the University of Missouri (1960s)
Interesting B: Performed Buck Buchanan's wedding and delivered Len Dawson's funeral eulogy.
Interesting C: Attended an SCLC meeting with Daddy King (MLK Sr.). Named KC civil rights monument alongside other MNF members
Family / Wife: Wife: Janice. Multiple grandchildren including a granddaughter who inspired his work in youth public health.
Career: Public health career: ran a neighborhood health clinic, oversaw prenatal care programs, community housing work. Founded 'Combat' drug treatment program (4% recidivism rate). Ran for Kansas City mayor. Intellectual leader of MNF; weekly column author for KC Call.

Name: Emanuel Cleaver
Interesting A: Family is split across two wings of the civil rights movement: Emanuel founded the KC chapter of the SCLC (Southern Christian Leadership Conference); cousin Eldridge Cleaver was a Black Panther and author of 'Soul on Ice.'
Interesting B: Performed Carl Peterson's (Chiefs GM) wedding ceremony.
Interesting C: Listed on Kansas City civil rights monument alongside Ike Gardner and Charles Allen.
Career: United Methodist minister at St. James. Kansas City Mayor. U.S. Congressman. Civil rights organizer. SCLC founder (KC chapter).

Name: Jerry Price
Interesting A: Only high school and college coach known to score over 100 points in a single football game in both high school AND college (Langston University). His high school team once scored 666 points in 10 games.
Interesting B: Brought Buck Buchanan, Otis Taylor, and Len Dawson to his high school practice sessions.
Interesting C: Recruited into MNF ~1972 by Ike Gardner after living in the same apartment complex (Linwood Hills, ~30th Street).
Career: Coach of football, girls basketball, and track at Lincoln High and Van Horn (combined ~32 years). Sent over 80 students to college. Kansas City Coaches Association head coaching award (2003). Also taught drafting.

Name: Lloyd Greenfield
Interesting A: Survived a catastrophic explosion at or near his former fire station that killed his entire crew. The pressure cracked a mirror in his home bedroom.
Interesting B: Also worked as a real estate agent (helped Ed West find his house upon returning from California).
Interesting C: Voted into MNF at Charles Allen's house — he was not an original member but was welcomed by formal vote.
Family / Wife: Wife: (also a Lincoln High School grad, class of 1959). Mentored and tutored a grandniece through high school; she earned a scholarship to University of Missouri.
Career: Kansas City Fire Department (~29 years). Post office worker before that. Real estate agent after retirement.

Name: Charles Allen III
Interesting A: Owned three consecutive jazz clubs: Club Sands (named after the Sands in Las Vegas, where Sammy Davis Jr. played), Birdland (named after Charlie Parker), and Mardi Gras. Wilt Chamberlain (then at KU) regularly visited the Chicken Shack — coming in Fridays after school in his big Buick or Oldsmobile.
Interesting B: Attended Lincoln University in Jefferson City with Ollie Hubbard and Georgia Buchanan (Buck Buchanan's wife) — a key link between MNF and the Chiefs.
Interesting C: Worked at Niles Home for Boys at night while attending dental school.
Family / Wife: Wife: Nellie. Daughter (helped prepare food at meetings). Known for his seven-layer salad and cooking at MNF meetings.
Career: Dentist (DDS). Ran dental office in the Lincoln Building at 18th & Vine. Ran a nonprofit that hosts annual fundraising party at Armacost Museum in Grandview, using proceeds for scholarships to young men. Also worked at Swope Park/KC Zoo security.

Name: Harry Sypert ("That Harry")
Interesting A: Was almost drafted to Vietnam but his summer USDA Soil Conservation Service work counted as prior military service.
Interesting B: Dedicated Raiders fan — so much so that Carl Banks made him two custom Raiders jackets (one presented at Niles Home).
Interesting C: Participated in Langston University's civil rights awakening in the late 1960s — classmates included activists from Newark, Chicago, and the Bay Area who boycotted the cafeteria the night after MLK was killed.
Family / Wife: His sister also attended Langston and was in the band.
Career: History teacher at Lincoln High School (first job: all-freshman world history). Homeschool coordinator for KC School District Pupil Services (6+ schools). Worked at Niles Home for Boys (started 1978, after divorce). Coached football 32 years.

Name: John Martin
Interesting A: Rescued Kansas testing scores as superintendent — turned around a struggling district's academic performance.
Interesting B: Joined MNF ~2009-2010, introduced by Charles Allen after their wives worked together on the Obama campaign.
Interesting C: Received a curator scholarship to University of Missouri. Originally wanted to be an engineer.
Family / Wife: Wife (Met Allen through wives' shared Obama campaign volunteer work.)
Career: Superintendent of Schools (led turnaround of district testing scores). Director of Personnel for school district before that. PhD. Currently helps Nunnelly explain technical topics at MNF meetings ('Jim assigns me things he wants explained').

Name: Jason Joseph
Interesting A: Inherited the civil rights legacy of the Kansas City Call — the paper was founded October 1919 by C.A. Franklin; his great-aunt was Lucille Bluford's protégé and constant companion. Bluford was the reporter who documented Jim Crow and WWII civil rights.
Interesting B: The Call was involved in Brown v. Board of Education in Topeka. Under Joseph's watch, MNF column (~500 articles) became the longest-running column in the paper's history — no other columnist has lasted as long.
Interesting C: Describes Nunnelly as one of his mentors. Calls Jim one of '17 mentees' he counts.
Family / Wife: Mother: Gloria Joseph (owner of the KC Call Newspaper)
Career: VP / Operations Manager, Kansas City Call newspaper. Manages relationship with MNF column. Manages editorial decisions around when (and when not) to push MNF toward political endorsements.

Name: Don Williams
Interesting A: First (and only) white member of MNF. Found the group by reading the Kansas City Call and walked into Bob's in Motion bar asking for Carl Clark.
Interesting B: Shares Oakland Raiders fandom with Harry Sypert.
Career: Retired Air Force pilot who flew alongside Tuskegee Airman John Briggs. Also worked at Western Electric (same employer as Ed West). Active in his church's community work. Joined MNF ~2022, toward the end of COVID.

Name: Ed West
Interesting A: Pioneer at Western Electric — one of the first Black supervisors at a company hiring aggressively during the equal opportunity era. Nunnelly credits him with helping create early computers.
Interesting B: Hosted famous New Year's Eve parties in Kansas City. His son Damon West became a music producer in Los Angeles (California era). West also organized large community events — one attracted the KC mayor (Cleaver) and Buck Buchanan.
Interesting C: Served as interim director of the Kansas City Office of Citizens Complaints (civilian police review board) upon returning from California — an appointed position during a leadership vacancy.
Family / Wife: Wife: Celeste (worked for General Motors). Son: Damon (music producer, LA). Multiple children including one who became director of recreation for the City of San Diego.
Career: Western Electric computer pioneer (supervisor, then supervisor of supervisors). Langston-trained athlete (competed in multiple college sports). Interim director, Office of Citizens Complaints, Kansas City. Also worked in KC school system.

Name: C. Artis Ashley
Interesting A: No longer with us — no further details in transcripts.

Name: Mila Banks
Interesting A: Only woman member noted in the CSV

Name: Paul Booker
Interesting A: In his obituary, Booker listed the Monday Night Footballers directly alongside his family members — the only known MNF member to do so. Jerry Price: 'Paul was always on my side.'
Interesting B: Worked at Niles Home for Boys alongside Ike Gardner, Walt Livingston, Harry Sypert, and Charles Allen — a key institutional bond.
Career: Kansas City School District employee. Niles Home for Boys staff. Strong presence in MNF social cohesion.

Name: Tom Brown
Interesting A: Known to later members through the KC Call column.
Career: Possibly a sports writer (may have worked for the KC Star or KC Call — Allen was uncertain).

Name: Tony (Mac) Clay
Interesting A: Murdered — shot and killed. Carl Clark mentions his death as one of the losses within the group. Killed approximately 15 years before the 2025 interviews (c. 2010).
Interesting B: Had not been with MNF long before his death. Allen describes him as a sports writer who worked for the Star or the Call.
Interesting C: His murder was one of the traumatic losses the group absorbed together.
Career: Sports writer (KC Star or KC Call, per Allen — unverified). Short-tenure MNF member.

Name: Ollie Hubbard
Interesting A: Original founding member. Attended Lincoln University in Jefferson City alongside Charles Allen and Georgia Buchanan (Buck Buchanan's wife) — a key link to the Chiefs.
Interesting B: A larger, athletic man who played football. One of the closest friends of Oscar Jamison.
Career: Unknown career. Lincoln University attendee. Lincoln High School alum.

Name: Oscar Jamerson (Jameson — common misspelling)
Interesting A: Likely hosted the very first MNF meeting at his home on Gregory Street, across from the graveyard (per Ed West's and Dave Tell's accounts).
Interesting B: He and Ollie Hubbard were especially close — both larger, athletic men who played football at Lincoln High.
Interesting C: In the same Lincoln High class as Charles Allen.
Career: Unknown career. Lincoln High School alum. Possible first host of MNF meetings.

Name: Walt Livingston ("Uncle Walt")
Interesting A: One of the original MNF members. Worked at Niles Home for Boys — a key institutional bond connecting him to Ike Gardner, Paul Booker, Harry Sypert, and Charles Allen.
Interesting B: Also known as 'Bunny Rabbit' (Stuart Watson is listed separately with that nickname — possible confusion in original CSV; Allen uses 'Bunny Rabbit' for Watson at Lincoln University).
Interesting C: Recreation director — Allen confirms Walt was involved in recreation work.
Career: Recreation director. Niles Home for Boys staff. Lincoln High School alum.

Name: Isaac (Ike) Gardner
Interesting A: The social and emotional center of MNF — described by Mrs. Greenfield as 'the papa.' Grew up with Carl Clark (best friends from childhood, though different grade schools). Became assistant superintendent of KCPS. Named on Leon Jordan Civil Rights Memorial.
Interesting B: His loud, argumentative personality — especially his constant debates with Nunnelly — was described as the living engine of the group. His death left a void that multiple interviewees grieve.
Interesting C: active method to the protests' — Ike was involved in civil rights direct action in Kansas City.
Family / Wife: Wife (unnamed). Clark describes Ike 'and his wife' joining them at parties; the two couples socialized together for decades.
Career: Schoolteacher, then administrator in Kansas City Public Schools (eventually assistant superintendent). Niles Home for Boys supervisor. Civil rights activist.

Name: Stuart Watson (Stewart Watson IV) ("Bunny Rabbit")
Interesting A: Parents controlled the third floor of the Lincoln Building at 18th & Vine — a prestigious address that housed Black physicians and dentists.
Interesting B: Attended Lincoln University in Jefferson City with Charles Allen and Ollie Hubbard. Allen knew him as 'Bunny Rabbit.'
Interesting C: Clark knew Watson well before he moved to St. Louis.
Family / Wife: Family held prominent position in Kansas City's Black professional district (Lincoln Building).
Career: Unknown personal career. Family real estate/property connection at Lincoln Building.

Name: Ollie Gates
Interesting A: Hosts the Monday Nighters — Gates BBQ is a confirmed gathering place for the group. Nunnelly visits every Thursday.
Interesting B: The Gates BBQ family is one of Kansas City's most prominent Black business dynasties — a community institution.
Career: Restaurateur / Gates BBQ proprietor. Community anchor for MNF and the broader KC Black community.

Name: Wayne Miner
Interesting A: No details found in transcripts.

Name: Samuel Rogers
Interesting A: No details found in transcripts.
